message.content: It is a very common trap to just average the two speeds, but average speed actually depends on the total distance divided by the total time of the trip. Since the driver is going much faster on the way back, will they spend more time driving at 30 mph or at 60 mph?

message.content: So to sum up, you have completely mastered this concept: average speed is always the total distance divided by the total time, rather than just the average of the two speeds. When you spend more time driving at one speed, the overall average gets pulled toward that speed, but if the travel times are equal, the average sits right in the middle. You did fantastic work reasoning through this tricky math today!
